mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which raises corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between month-over-month INDPRO changes and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 9 percent indicates severe labor market slack that suppresses wage growth and consumer spending, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of months when UNRATE exceeds 9.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signal synchronized consumer and residential investment demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST rise month-over-month, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in fewer than 55 percent of instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero lower bound regime where the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is at or below 0.25 percent, a rising consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) pushes the real policy rate lower, expanding equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods when FEDFUNDS is at or below 0.25 percent and CPIAUCSL rises month-over-month, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) below 600 thousand units mark a depressed residential construction sector that drags on GDP and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of months when HOUST is below 600 thousand units.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in payroll employment (PAYEMS) during an accommodative federal funds rate regime produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days because labor market healing occurs without monetary tightening risk.
regime_hint: accommodative policy with labor market improvement
failure_condition: When PAYEMS increases month-over-month while FEDFUNDS is at or below 0.25 percent, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive relative to baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Year-over-year consumer price index inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 3.5 percent erodes corporate profit margins and real household income, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year change exceeds 3.5 percent, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals deteriorating labor income and collapsing aggregate demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When PAYEMS falls month-over-month, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of instances.
